mnemonics: R.I.P. Threads: You can't Resume, Interrupt, or Pause them directly. Use cooperation instead.
Deprecated is a big red STOP sign: Don't use the `stop()` and `resume()` methods!
keyPoints: The `resume()` method is deprecated and unsafe because it does not allow the thread to release locks, potentially causing deadlocks.
The proper way to pause and resume a thread's execution is by using wait/notify mechanisms or higher-level abstractions like a flag variable checked in a loop.
A thread cannot be restarted after it terminates. The `start()` method can only be called once on a thread object.
commonMistakes: Attempting to use the deprecated `Thread.resume()` method instead of implementing a cooperative thread control mechanism.
Calling `start()` on a thread instance a second time to try to 'restart' it after it has finished running, which throws an `IllegalThreadStateException`.
Forgetting to synchronize properly on the lock object when using `wait()` and `notify()`, leading to unpredictable behavior or `IllegalMonitorStateException`.
